mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth over a 5-year rolling window (p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:historical inverse correlation between low unemployment and future equity returns
failure_condition: If SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0% over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising significantly while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to inflation, which typically supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:real interest rates impact equity valuations
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to generate positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, a sudden deceleration (INDPRO) indicates a potential slowdown in manufacturing activity, which can signal broader economic weakness and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month change < 0.1% after 3 consecutive months of > 0.5% growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 does not exhibit negative 5-day returns in at least 55% of instances where INDPRO growth decelerates sharply during an expansionary regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns show a statistically insignificant or negative correlation with PAYEMS growth over any 7-year rolling window (p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical level indicates a weakening housing market, which can have ripple effects across the economy, signaling broader demand contraction and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 1000 (thousands of units)
evidence_refs: fact:housing market as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 does not experience negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of periods following a sustained drop in HOUST below 1000 units.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising rapidly while GDP growth (GDPC1) is decelerating, it suggests a monetary tightening cycle impacting a slowing economy, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon due to higher discount rates and reduced earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.5% and GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:monetary policy impact on economic growth
regime_hint: monetary tightening in a slowing economy
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises significantly while GDPC1 growth is weak.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a strong rebound in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates renewed consumer confidence and spending, which provides a tailwind for corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month growth > 1.0% after a period of negative growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 does not show positive 5-day returns in at least 65% of triggered instances during a post-recession recovery phase.
